evidence.guard: promises.length > 0 check at line 213 — section is entirely suppressed if no promises exist
evidence.counts: fulfilled/broken/expired/active filtered at lines 225-228 from state.run.promises
evidence.addPromiseRow: internal helper at lines 230-243 — skips count=0 rows (no ghost entries)
evidence.colours: fulfilled=#7cb87c (green), broken=#b87c7c (red), expired=#cd853f (amber), active=#a0a0b8 (grey) — semantically correct
evidence.divider_header: hr + 'Promesses' section header inserted after factions block, before the section

evidence.logic: incrementCardsPlayed now runs a map() over run.promises before returning new state
evidence.transition: spreads p with status:'expired' as const — immutable, type-safe
evidence.placement: expiry fires on the same tick that cardsPlayed increments — semantically correct (card N finishes, deadline check runs)

evidence.covers_negative: Math.max(1, ...) wraps the full parseInt+fallback chain — negative values (-3, -99) are floored to 1
evidence.covers_zero: parseInt('0') returns 0, '|| 5' fallback fires, then Math.max(1, 5)=5 — zero becomes 5
evidence.closes_c65_issue: C65 MEDIUM issue: 'ADD_PROMISE:id:-3 stores deadlineCards=-3' — fully resolved

evidence.trigger: applied only when dots.length > 1 — single dots retain role='img' on the dot itself
evidence.closes_c65_issue: C65 LOW issue: 'Multi-dot container span has no role or aria-label' — fully resolved

notes: All four changes maintain the immutable spread pattern throughout.
incrementCardsPlayed expiry: map() returns a new array — RunState.promises is readonly, contract respected.
RunSummary: promises.length guard prevents rendering when state is empty; addPromiseRow skips count=0 — no ghost UI elements.
Remaining known-stable minor: FULFILL_PROMISE with empty-string id could match an empty-id promise. Low-probability, not a regression from C65.
No new error-paths introduced. No crash vectors identified.
The two C65 MEDIUM issues are resolved: negative deadline guard (Math.max(1,...)) and expiry engine (incrementCardsPlayed).
Expiry semantics are correct: deadline is relative to madeAtCard (not absolute), so a 5-card promise made at card 10 expires at card 15. Consistent with Game Design Bible T050 promise window.
addPromiseRow hides zero-count categories — player sees only relevant outcome types, not a list of empty rows.

RESIDUAL: Duplicate promise id accumulation (C65 LOW, not addressed in C66). addPromise still pushes without dedup. fulfillPromise targets first matching active only — second instance stays active and will later expire. Semantically odd if the same logical promise is pushed twice, but no card in cards.json does this currently.
RESIDUAL: FULFILL/BREAK on an already-expired promise is a no-op (status !== 'active' guard). Correct behaviour if a card fires FULFILL after the deadline passed.
C65 LOW issue resolved: multi-dot container now has role='group' + aria-label='Impacts de faction'.
Screen readers now announce: 'Impacts de faction, group' then enumerate 'druides (+5)', 'ankou (-3)' etc. as img elements. Ergonomic grouping achieved.
RunSummary promise section uses plain div/span with textContent — no innerHTML, no XSS vector.
Promise row labels are French prose ('Promesses tenues', 'Promesses rompues', 'Promesses expirees', 'Promesses ouvertes') — readable by screen readers without further ARIA annotation needed.
Colour coding (green/red/amber/grey) carries semantic meaning duplicated in text labels — WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) satisfied.
Promises are now fully visible to the player at run end — moral accountability loop is closed.
Colour semantics match narrative tone: green (tenues/fulfilled), red (rompues/broken), amber (expirees — time-lapsed, morally ambiguous), grey (ouvertes — still pending).
The 'expired' category surfaced in RunSummary adds narrative texture: a promise that lapsed without resolution implies abandonment, which is a distinct moral statement from being explicitly broken.
Section only renders when promises.length > 0 — runs without promise cards are uncluttered.
Still missing: no promise details (what was promised to whom). Counts are correct but anonymous. Future improvement: store a faction or target label in the Promise object.
This is the primary target criterion for C66 — the +2.0 delta is the expected recovery.
Promises are now a complete mini-loop: ADD_PROMISE in card effect → tracked in state → expires if unresolved → surfaced in RunSummary with outcome colour.
The expiry engine means players can now experience the promise system without relying on LLM-generated FULFILL/BREAK effects — any promise made will resolve one way or another.
Moral weight is player-visible: seeing '2 Promesses rompues, 1 Promesse expiree' at run end creates emotional feedback.
MINOR: Promise narrative text (what the promise was about) is not stored. The summary shows counts only. This is a future narrative-depth task, not a blocker.
Overall: the promise system is now a functional, player-facing feature.
Math.max(1, parseInt(...) || 5) is the most defensive form of the deadline parse — all malformed LLM outputs now produce a valid positive integer.
ADD_PROMISE:id (missing deadline arg) is still rejected by VALID_CODES check (expects 2 args) before reaching the Math.max guard — the guard only handles the 2-arg path. Correct layered defence.

Promise id is still LLM-free-text. A hallucinated id that never matches FULFILL/BREAK will now expire via the new engine rather than accumulating as permanent 'active' state. This is a meaningful improvement to LLM-resilience.
FULFILL/BREAK on a wrong id still silently no-ops — no degradation.
incrementCardsPlayed expiry is co-located with the cardsPlayed increment — single responsibility maintained.
addPromiseRow is a pure local helper inside showRunSummary — no export pollution.
RunSummary section is behind a promises.length guard, keeping the DOM construction path clean for runs without promises.
No new imports added in any of the four changed files.
STYLE: addPromiseRow uses void return type implicitly — consistent with the file's existing addStat helper pattern.
incrementCardsPlayed now does one extra map() per card played — O(n) on promises array where n ≤ 10 in practice. Negligible.
RunSummary promise section is rendered once at run end, not per-frame. No perf concern.
No new requestAnimationFrame or setTimeout calls introduced.
No minigame changes in C66 — score carries forward.
Promise expiry now fires on incrementCardsPlayed, which is called after each card regardless of minigame outcome — expiry is correctly decoupled from minigame success/failure.
Four focused changes across four files. Minimal blast radius.
Commit message 'feat(cycle-66): promise RunSummary display, expiry engine, deadline guard, ARIA group' accurately summarises all four changes.
No new dependencies, no new files — changes are additive to existing modules.

composite_score.delta_note: Exact recovery target reached. C63=8.5, C64=8.3, C65=7.9, C66=8.5. The +2.0 on progression_fun_factor (6.5→8.5) drives the recovery. equilibrage also jumps +1.3 (7.5→8.8) from the expiry engine. accessibilite gains +0.5 from the ARIA group fix.

verdict_rationale: All 4 C66 changes verified as correctly implemented. No regressions. The promise system is now a complete, player-facing feature: state tracks active/fulfilled/broken/expired, expiry fires on card increment, deadline guard prevents negative windows, and RunSummary surfaces outcome counts with colour-coded moral framing. Composite 8.5 meets the ≥8.5 PROCEED threshold exactly.

issues.issue: Duplicate promise ids can accumulate in state. addPromise does not check for existing active promises with the same id. If a card fires ADD_PROMISE twice with the same id, fulfillPromise will only resolve the first instance.
issues.recommendation: Add an upsert guard in Store.addPromise: if an active promise with the same id already exists, overwrite its deadlineCards rather than pushing a duplicate. Or document the 'multiple instances allowed' intent explicitly.

issues.issue: RunSummary shows promise counts only (fulfilled: 2, broken: 1) with no description of what was promised. The player knows their moral score but not the moral content.
issues.recommendation: Add a text label field to the Promise interface (e.g. label: string) populated at ADD_PROMISE time from the card narrative. Display in RunSummary as '2x Promesse tenue: proteger les korrigans'. This is a narrative-depth enhancement, not a blocker.

top3_c67.rationale: Highest remaining fun-factor delta available. Counts without context (2 broken, 1 expired) are less emotionally resonant than named promises. Adding a label field to Promise and surfacing it in RunSummary would push coherence_narrative from 8.5 toward 9.0 and progression_fun_factor from 8.5 toward 9.0.

top3_c67.task: Promise mechanical consequences — secondary effects on FULFILL/BREAK (e.g. ADD_REPUTATION delta, ADD_ANAM bonus/penalty)
top3_c67.rationale: Currently fulfilling or breaking a promise changes only the status label. Adding optional secondary effects (FULFILL_PROMISE:id → triggers +10 druides reputation) would give the system mechanical weight beyond narrative tracking. Lifts equilibrage and progression_fun_factor.

top3_c67.task: Promise dedup guard + minigame coverage audit
top3_c67.rationale: Closes the one remaining LOW equilibrage issue (duplicate id accumulation). Bundle with a minigame coverage pass: couverture_minijeux has been static at 7.5 for three cycles. A targeted audit of which minigames lack test coverage or UX issues could unlock a +0.3 gain.

balance_observations: Promise expiry window default (5 cards) is now enforced as a floor via Math.max(1,...). No balance tuning needed yet.
The expired category appearing in RunSummary creates implicit pressure on players to resolve promises quickly — this is a desirable tension mechanic, not a bug.

fulfillPromise/breakPromise secondary effects (C67 rank-2) will be the first time promises have numerical balance parameters. Recommend starting with small deltas (+5 rep, +2 anam for fulfill; -3 rep for break) and tuning via playtest.
